title: Pachystelis dubia
taxonomy: Pachystelis dubia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This family is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The Acanthaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Pachystelis dubia belongs to one of them. Within the family Acanthaceae, Pachystelis dubia is part of the tribe Rupestres. More specific classification details may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be within this family and subfamily.
uses: Pachystelis dubia has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin infections.
